description:
Goal
Compiler2 should do runtime work only for values that are demanded by an executable boundary. Exact callable use is semantic evidence, not representation obligation. The compiler must preserve the exact callable surfaces it already discovers and derive all internal transport, ABI lanes, callable entries, native callable boundaries, and closure materialization only from settled runtime demand.

Why this replaces the narrow bug
The old symptom was ABI-ready programs publishing callable_entries for closure bodies even when no call or escape demanded them. That is not a local ABI bug. It is a modeling bug: compiler2 discovers exact callable use, throws it away, then later recovers runtime obligations from types or from narrower transport models. That recovery is inherently too broad and will keep producing unused callable constructors, extra callable entries, wrong closure surfaces, ABI leaks, and, as we have now proven, nested direct-callable transport bugs.

Core distinction
Semantic fact: which callable surface is actually used? The source of truth is CallSiteSummary.targets[*].surface_inputs plus exact escape/opaque evidence.
Representation fact: which callable values or structural lanes must physically exist at runtime? This is derived later from runtime demand.

Correct model
Add a root/executable-scoped runtime demand plan. ActivationKey.input remains semantic. Semantic activation inputs do not imply runtime ABI lanes. Runtime transport layout is derived from demand after semantic closure is sealed.

Demand vocabulary
RuntimeDemand = Ignore | Value | TupleFields(Vec<RuntimeDemand>) | Callable(CallableDemand).
CallableDemand preserves exact demand as resolved surfaces, opaque, and escape. Resolved(surface_inputs) means a direct callable invocation needs that callable surface. Opaque means the callable reaches a boundary that can call it with unknown shape. Escape means a first-class callable value must survive as data.

Transport rule
There must be one shared internal transport model for runtime-carried values. Inputs, direct call args, continuation captures, executable returns, and delivered-resume inputs must all use the same demand-derived recursive layout family. The layout family must be able to represent at least:
- omitted
- one runtime value lane
- tuple fields as recursively described subvalues
- direct callables with exact target identity and recursively described captures

The exact Rust names can differ, but the invariant is mandatory: internal return/resume transport may not collapse back to a narrower model than input transport, and direct callable captures may not be flattened into raw ABI lanes before a concrete boundary needs them.

Representation derivation
Only after runtime demand settles, derive:
- internal boundary transport layouts
- ABI-ready callable entries
- native callable boundaries
- backend/native/codegen closure materialization

Direct-only resolved callable demand may pass executable identity plus demanded recursive captures without allocating or publishing a first-class closure. Opaque or escaping callable demand requires first-class callable materialization and a callable-entry boundary.

What we now know
The first half of the plan is working: inputs, returns, and resumes can all be carried by one shared layout family. The remaining red proved the missing seam. The first wrong representation now appears in artifact-side callable witness/layout derivation, where nested direct-only callable captures are still flattened into capture types and ABI repr vectors. Native only exposes the contradiction later. This means the root fix is data-model-up: preserve recursive callable producer structure, derive recursive layouts from demand, then let backend/native consume those facts.

Removal target
Remove callable-specific and boundary-specific recovery paths:
- runtime_callable_values, runtime_callable_boundary_values, note_callable_arg_uses, ClosureBoundaryUse::CallableArg, closure_value_needs_runtime_boundary, resolve_materialized_runtime_callable_boundary_activations
- ABI-ready callable entry derivation from arbitrary callable value types
- native callable boundary selection from capture types
- native callable identity collection from all callable-producing steps
- backend/native special-case transport recomputation once shared recursive boundary transport exists
- the assumption that executable returns can be modeled by a smaller transport vocabulary than inputs
- flat direct-callable capture metadata that stores only capture types / ABI reprs instead of recursive structure

Signal
Existing quicksort tuple-field delivery must remain green. compiler2_abi_ready_does_not_publish_unused_callable_constructors must pass for the right reason: no demand, no callable entry. Enum.reduce and related closure-call fixtures must pass because direct-only callables stay direct across call, return, and resume boundaries, including nested callable captures. Telemetry should expose demand counts, omitted lanes, direct-callable transport, recursive capture structure, and first-class materialization separately.

Strategy
Work data-model-up. First model demand, then compute the demand closure, then derive one shared recursive boundary transport from that plan, then derive callable materialization and external publication from the same facts, then remove backend/native/interpreter recovery logic that was compensating for missing transport facts. Each child ticket is one green commit and closes after its checks pass.

Design reference
See proposal.html for the worked examples. Tickets below carry the required data structures, algorithms, removals, and checks so the plan survives without local context.
title: Compiler2 runtime value demand: derive ABI and callable work from demand
